content.problemStatement:
JJ is back with another challenge. He challenges you to construct an array AAA containing NNN integers such that the following conditions hold:


For all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, 1≤Ai≤1061 \le A_i \le 10^61≤Ai​≤106
Every subarray has non-zero XOR. That is, for every 1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N, AL⊕AL+1⊕…⊕AR≠0A_L \oplus A_{L+1} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_R \ne 0AL​⊕AL+1​⊕…⊕AR​=0. Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Can you complete JJ's challenge?
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the required size of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^6) such that every subarray XOR is non‑zero.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 the construction gives A1 = 1 xor 0 = 1. The only subarray XOR is 1, which is non‑zero.
Prefix XORs become 1,2,3,4,5,6 respectively, all distinct and non‑zero, so every subarray XOR is non‑zero.
First test case N=2 yields [1,3]; second test case N=5 yields [1,3,1,7,1]. In both cases prefix XORs are 1..N, guaranteeing the condition.

solution.approach: Let pref[i] be the XOR of the first i elements. If all pref[i] (i≥1) are distinct and none equals 0, then for any subarray L..R we have XOR = pref[R] xor pref[L‑1] ≠ 0 because pref[R] ≠ pref[L‑1]. A simple way to guarantee distinct prefix XORs is to set pref[i] = i (the integer i itself). Then Ai = pref[i] xor pref[i‑1] = i xor (i‑1). For i ≥ 1, i xor (i‑1) is always a positive odd number not larger than 2·⌊log2 i⌋+1 ≤ 131072 for i ≤ 100000, well inside the 10^6 limit. The construction works for any N and satisfies the required condition.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (just a linear scan to output the numbers).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        line = []
        for i in range(1, N+1):
            line.append(str(i ^ (i-1)))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(line))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
